verses.t: The copy of the Testament of Asher, what things he spake to his sons in the hundred and
twenty-fifth year of his life. For while he was still in health, he said to them: Hearken, ye children of Asher, to your father, and I will declare to you all that is upright in the sight of Yahuah.
Two ways hath Aluahim given to the sons of men, and two inclinations, and two kinds of action, and
two modes (of action), and two issues. Therefore all things are by twos, one over against the
other. For there are two ways of good and evil, and with these are the two inclinations in our
breasts discriminating them. Therefore if the nephesh take pleasure in the good (inclination), all its
actions are in righteousness; and if it sin it straightway repenteth. For, having its thoughts set upon righteousness, and casting away wickedness, it straightway overthroweth the evil, and uprooteth

the sin. But if it incline to the evil inclination, all its actions are in wickedness, and it driveth away the good, and cleaveth to the evil, and is ruled by Beliar; even though it work what is good,
he perverteth it to evil. For whenever it beginneth to do good, he forceth the issue of the action into evil for him, seeing that the treasure of the inclination is filled with an evil ruach.

1.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: A person then may with words help the good for the sake of the evil, yet the issue of the action
leadeth to mischief. There is a man who showeth no compassion upon him who serveth his turn in

evil; and this thing hath two aspects, but the whole is evil. And there is a man that loveth him that worketh evil, because he would prefer even to die in evil for his sake; and concerning this it is clear that it hath two aspects, but the whole is an evil work.
Though indeed he have love, yet is he wicked who concealeth what is evil for the sake of the good name, but the end of the action tendeth unto evil.
Another stealeth, doeth unjustly, plundereth, defraudeth, and withal pitieth the poor: this too
hath a twofold aspect, but the whole is evil. He who defraudeth his neighbour provoketh Aluahim, and sweareth falsely against the Most High, and yet pitieth the poor: Yahuah who commandeth the
law he setteth at nought and provoketh, and yet he refresheth the poor. He defileth the nephesh, and maketh gay the body; he killeth many, and pitieth a few: this, too, hath a twofold aspect, but the

whole is evil. Another committeth adultery and fornication, and abstaineth from meats, and when he fasteth he doeth evil, and by the power of his wealth overwhelmeth many; and notwithstanding his excessive wickedness he doeth the mitsvot: this, too, hath a twofold aspect, but the
whole is evil. Such men are hares; clean,- like those that divide the hoof, but in very deed are
unclean. For Aluahim in the tables of the mitsvot hath thus declared.

2.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: But do not ye, my children, wear two faces like unto them, of goodness and of wickedness; but

cleave unto goodness only, for Aluahim hath his habitation therein, and men desire it. But from wickedness flee away, destroying the (evil) inclination by your good works; for they that are double-faced serve not Aluahim, but their own lusts, so that they may please Beliar and men like unto themselves.

3.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: For good men, even they that are of single face, though they be thought by them that are double
faced to sin, are just before Aluahim. For many in killing the wicked do two works, of good and evil;

but the whole is good, because he hath uprooted and destroyed that which is evil. One man hateth the merciful and unjust man, and the man who committeth adultery and fasteth: this, too, hath a two fold aspect, but the whole work is good, because he followeth Yahuah's example, in that he

accepteth not the seeming good as the genuine good. Another desireth not to see a good day with them that riot, lest he defile his body and pollute his nephesh: this, too, is double- faced, but the whole is

good. For such men are like to stags and to hinds, because in the manner of wild animals they seem to be unclean, but they are altogether clean; because they walk in zeal for Yahuah and abstain from what Aluahim also hateth and forbiddeth by His mitsvot, warding off the evil from the good.

4.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: Ye see, my children, how that there are two in all things, one against the other, and the one is hidden by the other: in wealth (is hidden) covetousness, in conviviality drunkenness, in laughter
grief, in wedlock profligacy. Death succeedeth to life, dishonour to glory, night to day, and darkness to light; [and all things are under the day, just things under life, unjust things under
death;] wherefore also eternal life awaiteth death. Nor may it be said that truth is a lie, nor

right wrong; for all truth is under the light, even as all things are under Aluahim. All these things, therefore, I proved in my life, and I wandered not from the truth of Yahuah, and I searched out the mitsvot of the Most High, walking according to all my strength with singleness of face unto that which is good.

5.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: Take heed, therefore, ye also, my children, to the mitsvot of Yahuah, following the truth
with singleness of face. For they that are double-faced are guilty of a twofold sin; for they both do the evil thing and they have pleasure in them that do it, following the example of the ruchot of
deceit, and striving against mankind. Do ye, therefore, my children, keep the law of Yahuah, and give not heed unto evil as unto good; but look unto the thing that is really good, and keep it in all
mitsvot of Yahuah, having your conversation therein, and resting therein. For the latter ends of men do show their righteousness (or unrighteousness), when they meet the mal'akim of the
Yahuah and of Satan. For when the nephesh departs troubled, it is tormented by the evil ruach which also it served in lusts and evil works.
But if he is peaceful with joy he meeteth the mal'ak of shalom, and he leadeth him into eternal life.

6.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: Become not, my children, as Sodom, which sinned against the mal'akim of Yahuah, and perished for
ever. For I know that ye shall sin, and be delivered into the hands of your enemies; and your land shall be made desolate, and your holy places destroyed, and ye shall be scattered unto the four

corners of the earth. And ye shall be set at nought in the dispersion vanishing away as water. Until the Most High shall visit the earth, coming Himself [as man, with men eating and drinking, and breaking the head of the dragon in the water. He shall save Yasharal and all the Gentiles [Aluahim speaking in
the person of man]. [Therefore do ye also, my children, tell these things to your children, that they
disobey Him not. For I have known that ye shall assuredly be disobedient, and assuredly act ungodly, not giving heed to the law of Aluahim, but to the mitsvot of men, being corrupted
through wickedness. And therefore shall ye be scattered as Gad and Dan my brethren, and ye shall
know not your lands, tribe, and tongue. But Yahuah will gather you together in faith through His tender mercy, and for the sake of Aḇraham, Yahtsḥaq, and Ya'aqoḇ.]

7.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf

verses.t: And when he had said these things unto them he commanded them, saying: Bury me in Hebron.
And he fell asleep and died at a good old age. And his sons did as he had commanded them, and they carried him up to Hebron, and buried him with his fathers.

8.pdf: THE TESTAMENTS OF THE TWELVE PATRIARCHS.pdf
